metadata.title: Gender differences in lipid and glucose kinetics during short-term fasting
metadata.journal: American Journal of Physiology - Endocrinology and Metabolism
metadata.study_design: Experimental study with stable isotope labeled tracers measuring whole body glycerol and glucose kinetics at 14 and 22 hours of fasting
metadata.demographics: Six men (32 ± 4 yr old) and six premenopausal women (27 ± 2 yr old), matched on percent body fat (24±2% and 23±2% body weight as fat, respectively)

evidence.quote: Gender differences in lipid and glucose kinetics during short-term fasting
BETTINA MITTENDORFER, JEFFREY F. HOROWITZ, AND SAMUEL KLEIN
Am J Physiol Endocrinol Metab 281: E1333–E1339, 2001
Six women and six men who were matched for adiposity (24±2 and 23±2% body wt as fat, respectively)
Substrate kinetics were measured by infusing stable isotope labeled tracers of glucose ([2H2]glucose) and glycerol ([2H5]glycerol)
Six men (32 ± 4 yr old) and six premenopausal women (27 ± 2 yr old), who were matched on percent body fat

methods_participants.label: Participant N and demographics
methods_participants.value: Six men (32 ± 4 yr old) and six premenopausal women (27 ± 2 yr old), who were matched on percent body fat

evidence.quote: Six men (32 6 4 yr old) and six premenopausal women (27 6 2 yr old), who were matched on percent body fat, participated in this study

methods_participants.label: Inclusion criteria - body weight stability
methods_participants.value: All subjects were considered to be in good health after completing a comprehensive medical evaluation that included a history and physical examination, an electrocardiogram, and standard blood and urine tests

evidence.quote: All subjects were considered to be in good health after completing a comprehensive medical evaluation that included a history and physical examination, an electrocardiogram, and standard blood and urine tests

methods_participants.label: Inclusion criteria - medications and smoking
methods_participants.value: In female subjects, the study was performed during the first 2 wk of the follicular phase of their menstrual cycle

evidence.quote: None of the subjects was taking regular medications or smoked tobacco

methods_participants.label: Body composition assessment method
methods_participants.value: Dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry (Hologic QDR 1000/w, Waltham, MA)

evidence.quote: Each subject's fat mass (FM) and fat-free mass (FFM) were determined by dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry (Hologic QDR1000/w, Waltham, MA)

methods_participants.label: Pre-study liquid formula - timing and composition
methods_participants.value: At 2000, subjects ingested a liquid formula (Ensure; Ross Laboratories, Columbus, OH), containing 250 kcal (40 g carbohydrates, 6.1 g fat, and 8.8 g protein)

evidence.quote: gender-related differences in body composition on subjects at 14 and 22 h of fasting
chilled tubes containing EDTA to determine plasma fatty acid, glycerol, and glucose concentrations and glycerol and glucose enrichments
Plasma was stored at 270°C until final analyses were performed

methods_participants.label: Fatty acid concentration measurement assay
methods_participants.value: Gas chromatography (Hewlett-Packard 5890-II, Palo Alto, CA) after adding heptadecanoic acid to plasma as an internal standard

evidence.quote: Plasma glucose concentration was determined by using an automated glucose analyzer (Yellow Springs Instrument, Yellow Springs, OH)

methods_participants.label: Statistical analysis method
methods_participants.value: Two-way analysis of variance (ANOVA), with gender and time as factors, followed by post hoc comparisons with Student's t-test for multiple comparisons

evidence.quote: A two-way analysis of variance (ANOVA), with gender and time as factors, was used to test for significant differences in substrate kinetics between men and women during fasting. Significant F-ratios were followed by post hoc comparisons with the use of Student's t-test for multiple comparisons
A value of P #0.05 was considered to be statistically significant

narrative_results.statement: The relative increase in glycerol Ra with continued fasting was blunted in women compared with men

evidence.quote: the relative increase in glycerol Ra with continued fasting was blunted in women compared with men (40±7 vs. 80±4% increase; P < 0.05)

narrative_results.statement: Sexual dimorphism exists in lipid, but not glucose, metabolism during both basal and short-term fasting conditions

evidence.quote: These data demonstrate the presence of sexual dimorphism in lipid, but not glucose, metabolism during both basal and short-term fasting conditions
